title: What My Daughter Taught Me
content: My daughter... [laughter] she's something else. When she was about fifteen, I was explaining compound interest to her. You know, trying to be a good dad. Showing her the spreadsheets, the charts. [pause] She looked at me and said: 'Dad, I get the math. What I don't get is why you work so much if the whole point is to have time later.' [pause] [laughter] I mean... I had a PhD in economics and my teenager just schooled me on the fundamental question of life. [pause] I didn't have a good answer. Still don't, honestly. But that question changed me. I started asking myself: what am I waiting for? What's the 'later' I'm saving for? [pause] Sometimes the wisest people in your life are the ones who haven't learned to stop asking the obvious questions.
follow_up: Now she's grown, has her own career. And you know what she told me recently? 'Dad, I get it now. It's hard.' [laughter] Circle of life, right?
lesson: The people who love you will ask the questions you're avoiding. Listen to them.
